title: Right now is enough
subtitle: A three-minute bedtime affirmation for the wired body looking for evidence the day went well.
excerpt: A short bedtime affirmation for the wired evening — the body lying down but the mind looking back at what didn't get done. Hand on the lower belly, three slow repetitions, then the same sentence in the dark until sleep arrives.
sourceNotes: Theme adapted from Day 8 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025), "Ground in the present moment". The specific sentence and the bedtime hand-on-belly framing are original to the homemade.education library.

practice.whenToUse: Use in bed on the wired evenings — body lying down but mind running back through what didn't get done, who you didn't reply to, what tomorrow needs.

content.text: A three-minute bedtime affirmation for the wired evening — body lying down, mind looking back through what didn't get done. One short sentence said with a hand on the lower belly, slow, in the dark.
Lie on your back. Hand flat on the lower belly, two fingers below the navel. Breathe into the hand, slow, until the belly rises against the palm.
Say the sentence three times, slow, with the breath. One repetition on each long exhale. The hand stays on the belly the whole time.

Then let the hand stay, and let the sentence repeat in your mind on each exhale until sleep arrives. If the mind wanders to tomorrow, bring it back to the sentence. The belly under the hand is the anchor.
If the wired feeling is loud enough that one sentence can't hold the attention, use
instead. The scan gives the attention more to move through.

The theme is adapted from Day 8 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025), which uses present-moment grounding as a bedtime wind-down. The specific sentence and the bedtime hand-on-belly framing are original to the homemade.education library.
